summary: First-finish backend smoke test for TruthLens AI covering auth, analyze/upload, history, analytics, trending, sources, chat, reports, profile, admin, and feedback endpoints. 28/29 checks passed; the one 'failure' was a test-script schema expectation mismatch (trending returns {trending:[...], total_articles:N} instead of a bare list). All core LLM-powered flows (analyze, upload, chat) worked end-to-end with gpt-5.2. No ObjectId (_id) leakage detected. Auth guards, admin role gating (403 for non-admin), and Google auth invalid-session 401 all behave correctly. Note: auth response field is `token` (not `access_token`) and reset-password expects `token` (not `reset_token`) — main agent should ensure frontend uses these field names.
passed: GET /auth/me returns current user with role
POST /auth/forgot-password returns reset_token in dev mode

POST /analyze returns full schema: prediction, confidence(50-99), credibility_score(0-100), risk_level, summary, reasoning, factors(8 keys), highlights, topics, recommendations, suspicious_statements, model_used, id
No ObjectId (_id) leakage in analyze response
POST /upload processes .txt file and returns analysis
GET /analytics returns all required aggregate keys
GET /source-rating for reuters.com -> Trusted, unknown domain -> Unknown
Protected endpoints return 401 without Bearer token
action_items: Informational only: auth endpoints return JWT under `token` field, and /auth/reset-password expects `token` (not `reset_token`). Ensure frontend uses these exact field names.
